title: LESSON 13: WHEN THEY WRITE TO YOU

content: This is the language of escalation.
Landlords, councils, tax offices,
and insurance companies use it. It
is designed to sound final.
It works. These words are intended
to pressure people into compliance.
But they all have plain meanings:
Forthwith = immediately
Pursuant = following
Deemed = officially considered
Null and void = cancelled
Once you know what they mean, the
power disappears. It is just
English in a suit. After this tier,
the language itself needn't faze you.
Quick writing rule: capital letters.
Capital letter after every full stop.
Capital letter for names of people and
places. Capital letter for months and days
of the week. Always capitalise 'I.'
The first word of every sentence always
starts with a capital letter. That is the
rule.
Do not capitalise random words for emphasis.
That is what bold text or underlining is for.
That covers 95% of capitalisation. You are
nearly done with the pack.
Press 8 to turn this lesson off
Congratulations. You are about to
see the 500th and final card of
English Advance.
You can fill in any form. You can
write a clear email. You can read
any official letter. You know the
words that trip everyone up and you
know the tricks to get them right.
500 words. All yours now.
English Adept (included free) is
the next step in the FlashBoss
ladder. 1,000 words that take you
from confident, to adept.
